description_long: This is a roughly hewn, rectangular stone block with an irregular, weathered surface and a muted beige-brown coloration. The block features some linear indentations and shallow grooves along its sides, suggesting either tool marks or decorative elements. Its surface is pitted and uneven, with visible chips and small inclusions of lighter material, indicating natural stone composition. The top and bottom faces are relatively flat but not perfectly level, while the sides are more irregular and undulating. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings. Such a block would typically be used as a barrier or boundary marker in an urban or historical setting.
description: A weathered, beige-brown stone block with rough, pitted surfaces and shallow linear grooves. Its irregular shape and solid build suggest use as a boundary or obstacle in urban environments.
description_view_0: The side view reveals the block's thickness and highlights two parallel grooves near the top edge, with a rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This angled view shows the broader face with pitted texture and some faint, shallow markings, as well as the irregular top contour.
description_view_2: The opposite side view again displays the parallel grooves and the block's thickness, with more visible chips and surface roughness.
description_view_3: The front face is broad and flat with a rectangular outline, showing the most surface weathering and some faint, indeterminate markings.
attribute_location_context: historic urban boundary or park landscaping
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set directly on compacted earth
attribute_weathering_pattern: The stone shows significant surface pitting, chipping, and discoloration, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements. Edges are rounded and some corners are chipped.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Faint, shallow linear grooves and indeterminate markings are visible, possibly from tools or weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Appears isolated but could be aligned with other blocks as part of a boundary or barrier.
